data.name: algal leaf in tea
data.cure: Improve air circulation by pruning bushes. Spray copper-based fungicides like Copper Oxychloride 50% WP. Maintain balanced plant nutrition to increase vigor.

data.name: anthracnose in tea
data.cure: Prune and destroy infected twigs. Spray with fungicides containing active ingredients like Chlorothalonil, Mancozeb, or copper-based compounds. Ensure proper drainage and avoid water stagnation.

data.name: apple apple scab
data.cure: Remove and dispose of fallen leaves in autumn to reduce fungal spores. Use resistant apple varieties. Apply fungicides like Captan, Myclobutanil, or Mancozeb, starting from early spring.

data.name: apple black rot
data.cure: Prune out dead or cankered branches during dormancy. Remove and destroy mummified fruits. Apply fungicides such as Captan or Thiophanate-methyl during the growing season.

data.name: apple cedar apple rust
data.cure: Remove nearby cedar or juniper trees (alternate hosts). If not possible, apply fungicides like Myclobutanil or Ferbam to apple trees from pink-bud stage through second cover spray.

data.name: apple healthy
data.cure: Maintain health through proper watering, balanced fertilization, regular pruning for air circulation, and proactive monitoring for pests and diseases.

data.name: bacterial leaf blight in rice leaf
data.cure: Plant resistant varieties. Use disease-free seeds. Avoid excessive nitrogen fertilization. Apply copper-based bactericides like Copper Hydroxide at the first sign of disease, though effectiveness can be limited.

data.name: bird eye spot in tea
data.cure: Improve air movement by pruning. Avoid excessive shade. Apply fungicides containing Copper Oxychloride or Mancozeb during periods of high humidity.

data.name: blight in corn leaf
data.cure: Use resistant hybrids. Practice crop rotation with non-host crops. Use tillage to bury crop residue. Apply foliar fungicides like Pyraclostrobin or Azoxystrobin if disease is severe.

data.name: blueberry healthy
data.cure: Maintain health by ensuring acidic soil (pH 4.5-5.5), regular watering, proper pruning, and applying appropriate fertilizers.

data.name: brown blight in tea
data.cure: Prune affected branches to improve air circulation. Spray protective fungicides such as Mancozeb or Copper Oxychloride, especially during wet seasons.

data.name: brown spot in rice leaf
data.cure: Ensure balanced nutrition, especially potassium. Treat seeds with fungicides like Thiram. Apply foliar fungicides such as Propiconazole or Azoxystrobin in the field.

data.name: cabbage looper
data.cure: Handpick caterpillars on small plantings. Use floating row covers to prevent egg-laying. Apply biological insecticides containing Bacillus thuringiensis (Bt) or Spinosad.

data.name: cercospora leaf spot
data.cure: Remove and destroy infected leaves. Improve air circulation. Apply fungicides containing Chlorothalonil, Myclobutanil, or copper-based compounds.

data.name: cherry (including sour) powdery mildew
data.cure: Prune trees to increase air circulation. Apply fungicides containing sulfur, potassium bicarbonate, or Myclobutanil at the first sign of mildew.

data.name: cherry (including_sour) healthy
data.cure: Maintain health with proper pruning, adequate watering, balanced fertilization, and monitoring for common pests and diseases like brown rot and aphids.

data.name: common rust in corn leaf
data.cure: Planting resistant hybrids is the primary method of control. Foliar fungicides like Pyraclostrobin or Tebuconazole can be effective if applied early when rust pustules first appear.

data.name: corn (maize) healthy
data.cure: Maintain health through appropriate planting density, timely weed control, balanced nutrient management (especially nitrogen), and adequate water supply.

data.name: corn crop
data.cure: Ensure optimal growth conditions including sufficient water, nutrients, and pest control to produce a healthy crop.

data.name: garlic
data.cure: Maintain health by planting in well-drained soil, ensuring full sun, and practicing crop rotation to prevent soil-borne diseases like white rot.

data.name: ginger
data.cure: Maintain health with well-drained, rich soil, partial shade, and consistent moisture. Avoid waterlogging to prevent rhizome rot.

data.name: grape black rot
data.cure: Practice good sanitation by removing and destroying mummified berries, infected leaves, and canes. Apply fungicides like Mancozeb, Ziram, or Myclobutanil on a regular schedule.

data.name: grape esca black measles
data.cure: There is no direct cure. Management involves pruning out and destroying symptomatic wood (cordons or trunk) well below the symptomatic area. Protect large pruning wounds with a sealant.

data.name: grape healthy
data.cure: Maintain health through a good spray program for diseases like powdery mildew and black rot, proper canopy management (leaf pulling), and balanced irrigation.

data.name: grape leaf blight isariopsis leaf spot
data.cure: Generally, this is a minor disease. Standard fungicide programs for other grape diseases (like black rot) using Mancozeb will also control leaf blight.

data.name: gray leaf spot in corn leaf
data.cure: Use tolerant corn hybrids. Practice crop rotation and tillage to manage residue. Apply foliar fungicides like Azoxystrobin with Propiconazole when lesions appear on lower leaves.

data.name: healthy tea leaf
data.cure: Maintain health by ensuring proper soil drainage and acidity, balanced fertilization, regular pruning, and managing pests and diseases proactively.

data.name: leaf smut in rice leaf
data.cure: This disease is usually minor and doesn't require control. Using disease-free seed and balanced fertilization helps. Fungicide application is not typically recommended.

data.name: lemon canker
data.cure: No cure for infected trees. Management focuses on prevention. Use windbreaks to reduce spread. Apply copper-based bactericides to protect fruit and leaves from infection.

data.name: nitrogen deficiency in plant
data.cure: Apply a nitrogen-rich fertilizer. For a quick fix, use a foliar spray of diluted liquid fertilizer like fish emulsion or a solution of Urea. For long-term treatment, amend the soil with compost or a granular fertilizer like Ammonium Sulfate.

data.name: onion
data.cure: Maintain health by ensuring well-drained soil, consistent watering, and practicing crop rotation to prevent diseases like downy mildew and pink root.

data.name: orange haunglongbing citrus greening
data.cure: There is no cure for infected trees. Management involves removing and destroying infected trees immediately and aggressively controlling the insect vector, the Asian citrus psyllid, with systemic insecticides like Imidacloprid.

data.name: peach healthy
data.cure: Maintain health with dormant oil sprays, regular pruning, and a preventative fungicide schedule to control major diseases like peach leaf curl and brown rot.

data.name: pepper bell bacterial spot
data.cure: Use disease-free seeds and resistant varieties. Practice crop rotation. Spray with copper-based bactericides combined with Mancozeb. Avoid working with plants when they are wet.

data.name: pepper bell healthy
data.cure: Maintain health by providing full sun, well-drained soil, consistent moisture, and monitoring for pests like aphids and diseases like bacterial spot.

data.name: potassium deficiency in plant
data.cure: Apply a potassium-rich fertilizer such as Muriate of Potash (Potassium Chloride) or Sulfate of Potash to the soil. For a faster response, a foliar spray of a soluble potassium fertilizer like Potassium Nitrate can be used.

data.name: potato crop
data.cure: Ensure a healthy crop by using certified seed potatoes, practicing crop rotation, and managing water and nutrient levels carefully to prevent disorders and diseases.

data.name: potato early blight
data.cure: Practice crop rotation. Destroy volunteer potato plants and weeds. Apply preventative fungicides containing Chlorothalonil, Mancozeb, or Azoxystrobin.

data.name: potato healthy
data.cure: Maintain health by planting certified disease-free seed, ensuring proper hilling of soil around plants, and using a preventative spray program for early and late blight.

data.name: potato hollow heart
data.cure: This is a physiological disorder, not a disease. Prevent it by maintaining consistent soil moisture and avoiding sudden growth spurts caused by heavy rain or excessive fertilization, especially late in the season.

data.name: potato late blight
data.cure: Use resistant varieties. Destroy all cull piles. Apply fungicides proactively, especially during cool, moist weather. Common fungicides include Chlorothalonil (preventative) and Cymoxanil (curative).

data.name: raspberry healthy
data.cure: Maintain health by pruning canes after harvest, ensuring good air circulation, managing weeds, and planting in well-drained soil to avoid root rot.

data.name: red leaf spot in tea
data.cure: This is often caused by nutrient imbalances or other stresses. Ensure balanced fertilization, particularly with potassium and magnesium. Improve soil health and drainage. Fungicides are generally not effective.

data.name: sogatella rice
data.cure: Control the insect vector, Sogatella furcifera (white-backed planthopper). Use resistant rice varieties. Apply systemic insecticides like Imidacloprid, Thiamethoxam, or Buprofezin when populations reach economic threshold levels.

data.name: soybean healthy
data.cure: Maintain health by using appropriate planting dates and row spacing, practicing crop rotation, using fungicide seed treatments, and scouting for pests like soybean aphids.

data.name: strawberry healthy
data.cure: Maintain health by planting in raised beds for drainage, using mulch (like straw) to keep fruit clean and reduce rot, and renovating beds after harvest.

data.name: strawberry leaf scorch
data.cure: Use resistant varieties. Remove old, infected leaves after harvest (renovation). Apply fungicides containing active ingredients like Myclobutanil or Captan.

data.name: tomato bacterial spot
data.cure: Use disease-free seed. Practice crop rotation. Mulch plants to reduce water splash. Spray with fixed copper bactericides, often mixed with Mancozeb for better results.

data.name: tomato canker
data.cure: There is no effective cure. Prevention is key. Use certified disease-free seed and transplants. Practice strict sanitation of tools. Rotate crops, avoiding tomatoes and peppers in the same spot for at least 3 years.

data.name: tomato early blight
data.cure: Prune lower leaves to increase air circulation. Mulch around base of plants. Apply preventative fungicides like Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb.

data.name: tomato healthy
data.cure: Maintain health by providing at least 6-8 hours of direct sun, consistent watering, staking or caging plants, and a balanced fertilizer low in nitrogen and high in phosphorus and potassium.

data.name: tomato late blight
data.cure: A very destructive disease. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately. Apply fungicides proactively, especially in cool, wet weather. Use products containing Chlorothalonil (preventative) or Cymoxanil (curative).

data.name: tomato leaf mold
data.cure: Improve air circulation by spacing plants and pruning. Reduce humidity, especially in greenhouses. Apply fungicides containing Mancozeb, Chlorothalonil, or copper-based compounds.

data.name: tomato septoria leaf spot
data.cure: Remove and destroy infected lower leaves. Use mulch to prevent spore splashing. Rotate crops. Apply fungicides containing Chlorothalonil or copper.

data.name: tomato spider mites two spotted spider mite
data.cure: Spray plants with a strong stream of water to dislodge mites. Use insecticidal soap or neem oil for light infestations. For severe cases, use a miticide containing active ingredients like Abamectin or Spiromesifen.

data.name: tomato target spot
data.cure: Improve air circulation and avoid overhead watering. Remove infected leaves. Apply fungicides like Chlorothalonil or Mancozeb.

data.name: tomato tomato mosaic virus
data.cure: No cure. Remove and destroy infected plants immediately to prevent spread. Practice good sanitation (wash hands, disinfect tools). Control insect vectors like aphids. Plant resistant varieties.

data.name: waterlogging in plant
data.cure: Improve soil drainage by adding organic matter (compost) or creating raised beds. For potted plants, ensure pots have drainage holes and use well-aerating potting mix. Reduce watering frequency.

data.name: coccidiosis
data.cure: This primarily affects poultry. Treat the flock with anticoccidial drugs (coccidiostats) in the water or feed, such as Amprolium or Toltrazuril. Keep litter clean and dry to prevent reinfection.

data.name: salmonella
data.cure: In animals (like poultry), treatment is often not practical for food production. Prevention is key: strict biosecurity, pest control, and using clean feed and water. In some cases, a veterinarian may prescribe antibiotics like sulfonamides.

data.name: healthy chicken
data.cure: Maintain health through a clean and dry coop, fresh water and appropriate feed, vaccinations, and biosecurity measures to prevent introduction of diseases.

data.name: chicken new castle disease
data.cure: No cure. This is a highly contagious viral disease. Prevention through vaccination is the only effective control method. Use vaccines like LaSota or B1 strains. Implement strict biosecurity to prevent introduction.
